json.comment: Synthetic metastatic NSCLC adenocarcinoma, never-smoker female 58, EGFR exon 19 deletion confirmed by tissue NGS. Engine routes ALGO-NSCLC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 (HIGH-RISK-BIOLOGY fires) → step 2 (EX19DEL fires) → IND-NSCLC-EGFR-MUT-MET-1L (osimertinib FLAURA, mPFS 18.9 mo).

Synthetic metastatic NSCLC adenocarcinoma, never-smoker male 47, ALK rearrangement (EML4-ALK variant 1) by FISH + RNA-NGS confirmation. Engine routes ALGO-NSCLC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 (HIGH-RISK-BIOLOGY fires via BIO-ALK-FUSION) → step 2 (EGFR not present) → step 3 (ALK fires) → IND-NSCLC-ALK-MET-1L (alectinib ALEX, mPFS 34.8 mo).

Synthetic metastatic NSCLC squamous, heavy ex-smoker male 68, PD-L1 TPS 85% (22C3 IHC), comprehensive NGS driver-negative. Engine routes ALGO-NSCLC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 (no driver, skip) → step 5 (PDL1≥50 fires) → IND-NSCLC-PDL1-HIGH-MET-1L (pembro mono KEYNOTE-024, mPFS 10.3 mo, 5-yr OS 31.9%).

Synthetic metastatic NSCLC adenocarcinoma non-squamous, female 62, PD-L1 TPS 25%, comprehensive NGS driver-negative. Engine routes ALGO-NSCLC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 (no driver, skip) → step 5 (PDL1<50, default) → IND-NSCLC-PDL1-LOW-NONSQ-MET-1L (pembro+carboplatin+pemetrexed KEYNOTE-189, mPFS 9.0 mo, 5-yr OS 19.4%).

Synthetic metastatic NSCLC adenocarcinoma, ex-smoker male 64, TMB 18 mut/Mb (TMB-high ≥10), PD-L1 TPS 15% (low), driver-negative on NGS. Engine routes ALGO-NSCLC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 (no driver) → step 5 (PDL1<50, default) → IND-NSCLC-PDL1-LOW-NONSQ-MET-1L (pembro+chemo). IND-NSCLC-TMB-HIGH-MET-1L-PEMBRO-MONO surfaces as alternative track for HCP based on TMB biomarker.

Synthetic metastatic NSCLC adenocarcinoma, female 54, EGFR L858R initially → 24 mo on erlotinib (1G TKI) → progression with acquired T790M on ctDNA + repeat tissue NGS. Line of therapy = 2. Engine algorithm correctly resolves ALGO-NSCLC-METASTATIC-2L step 1 (T790M fires) → step 2 (no ex20ins) → step 3 → IND-NSCLC-2L-EGFR-POST-OSI-AMI-LAZ (MARIPOSA-2 ami+laz). KB-DRIFT NOTE: post-filter currently drops POST-OSI-AMI-LAZ via overly-broad biomarker_requirements_excluded (the YAML excludes histologic transformation but the engine matches by BIO-EGFR-MUTATION ID alone), so default falls to IND-NSCLC-2L-EGFR-EX20INS-AMIVANTAMAB. Surfaces a real engine/KB issue worth a follow-up. Clinically: T790M post-1G/2G TKI → osimertinib 2L is the textbook path (AURA3); KB does not yet have a dedicated post-1G/2G osi-naïve T790M Indication.

prior_lines.outcome: PD with acquired T790M on ctDNA + tissue rebiopsy

json.comment: Synthetic metastatic NSCLC adenocarcinoma, female 51, EML4-ALK variant 1 → 22 mo on alectinib (2G TKI) 1L → CNS progression with new brain mets. Line of therapy = 2. prior_lines.outcome: CNS PD with new brain mets, extracranial stable

prior_lines.outcome: PD; ROS1 fusion identified on RNA-NGS rebiopsy

json.comment: Synthetic stage III colon adenocarcinoma, post-curative resection (R0), 62F, ECOG 0, intended adjuvant FOLFOX 6 mo (MOSAIC). KB DRIFT: there is currently NO adjuvant CRC algorithm in knowledge_base/hosted/content/algorithms/ — only ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-1L and ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-2L exist. The engine matches algorithms strictly by (disease_id, line_of_therapy), so a CRC line=1 patient — adjuvant or metastatic — routes through ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-1L and surfaces metastatic 1L tracks. The intended IND-CRC-ADJUVANT-STAGE3-FOLFOX never appears (it is in NO algorithm's output_indications). Engine actually routes step 4 default → IND-CRC-METASTATIC-1L-FOLFOX-BEV (clinically wrong for adjuvant; bevacizumab is not standard adjuvant per NSABP-C-08). This case is preserved as an audit artifact demonstrating the wired-vs-intended gap; the comment is the load-bearing piece, not the rendered plan. Fix requires authoring algo_crc_adjuvant.yaml referencing IND-CRC-ADJUVANT-STAGE3-FOLFOX (Phase-2 KB work, out of scope here).

Synthetic metastatic CRC, 58M, ECOG 1, left-sided sigmoid primary, extended-RAS WT (KRAS exons 2/3/4 + NRAS exons 2/3/4 all WT) + BRAF-WT + MSS. Engine routes ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 (no MSI-H) → step 2 (RF-CRC-RAS-WT fires + 'Left-sided primary (splenic flexure or distal)' literal-key clause truthy) → IND-CRC-METASTATIC-1L-RAS-WT-LEFT (FOLFOX + cetuximab, CRYSTAL/FIRE-3/CALGB-80405 left-sided benefit). Wiring note: the sidedness criterion is currently a free-text condition string evaluated as a literal finding-key lookup (no dedicated RF for primary-tumour location); patient profile sets that exact string truthy to fire step 2. A future RF-CRC-LEFT-SIDED-PRIMARY would be cleaner.

Synthetic metastatic CRC, 64M, ECOG 1, right-sided cecal primary, KRAS exon 2 G12V mutated (RAS-mutant excludes anti-EGFR per PRIME post-hoc), BRAF-WT, MSS. Engine routes ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 (no MSI-H) → step 2 (RF-CRC-RAS-WT does NOT fire because kras_mutation=true) → step 3 (no BRAF V600E) → step 4 (no frailty) → if_false default = IND-CRC-METASTATIC-1L-FOLFOX-BEV (FOLFOX + bevacizumab, NO16966/CALGB-80405 RAS-mut analysis). RF-CRC-RAS-MUTANT also fires (de-escalate direction = anti-EGFR removed from option set) but does not branch the algorithm. Right-sided + RAS-mut canonical bev+chemo target population.

Synthetic metastatic CRC, 51F, ECOG 0, right-sided ascending-colon primary, MSI-H / dMMR confirmed by IHC (MLH1/PMS2 loss) and PCR. Engine routes ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-1L step 1 → RF-CRC-MSI-H-ACTIONABILITY fires (msi_status=MSI-H, dmmr_status=deficient) → IND-CRC-METASTATIC-1L-MSI-H-PEMBRO (pembrolizumab monotherapy, KEYNOTE-177 mPFS 16.5 vs 8.2 mo over FOLFOX+bev). MSI-H supersedes RAS / BRAF / sidedness routing per algorithm step 1 priority. Lynch-syndrome workup recommended downstream (MLH1 promoter methylation testing → if absent, germline NGS panel).

Synthetic metastatic CRC 2L, 60M, ECOG 1, left-sided sigmoid primary, RAS-mutant (KRAS G12D), BRAF-WT, MSS. Disease progression on 1L FOLFOX+bev after 8 cycles (PR → PD at ~6 mo) with rapid CEA doubling. Engine routes ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-2L step 1 (no MSI-H) → step 2 (no BRAF) → step 3 (no KRAS-G12C) → step 4 (no HER2 amp) → step 5 (RAS-mut, no rechallenge) → step 6: RF-CRC-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION fires (cea_doubling_4_8wk=true) → IND-CRC-METASTATIC-2L-FOLFIRI-BEV (FOLFIRI continuation of bevacizumab, ML18147 / TML / E3200). Standard cytotoxic-switch + biologic-continuation track post-FOLFOX failure.

prior_lines.outcome: PD at 6 mo with rising CEA

json.comment: Synthetic chemo-refractory metastatic CRC, 67M, ECOG 1, left-sided sigmoid primary, RAS-mutant (KRAS G12V), BRAF-WT, MSS, post 1L FOLFOX+bev → 2L FOLFIRI+bev → PD; intent is 3L+ regorafenib (CORRECT, mOS 6.4 vs 5.0 mo HR 0.77, ReDOS dose-escalation strategy). KB DRIFT: there is currently NO ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-3L; the engine routes (DIS-CRC, line=3) to no algorithm and emits no plan. To preserve a renderable artifact this case is encoded as line_of_therapy=2 with prior_lines reflecting both 1L and 2L exposure, so it falls through ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-2L: step 1 (no MSI-H) → step 2 (no BRAF) → step 3 (no KRAS-G12C) → step 4 (no HER2 amp) → step 5 (RAS-mut, no rechallenge) → step 6 (RF-CRC-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION does NOT fire — no cea_doubling / peritoneal / oxaliplatin-relapse trigger) → step 7 if_false default = IND-CRC-METASTATIC-3L-TAS102-BEV. The engine surfaces TAS-102+bev (SUNLIGHT) as the ENGINE-DEFAULT track; IND-CRC-METASTATIC-3L-REGORAFENIB appears as one of the alternative tracks via algo.output_indications. Drift: the brief asked for regorafenib as the headline plan, but the wired KB prefers SUNLIGHT TAS-102+bev (better tolerability + slight OS edge) and demotes regorafenib to alternative-track positioning per the indication's known_controversies + Ukraine-context access analysis. Fix would require either an ALGO-CRC-METASTATIC-3L with regorafenib as a frailty/preference branch, or a dedicated RF-CRC-TAS102-INELIGIBLE gate — both are Phase-2 KB work.

Synthetic Stage IV cutaneous melanoma, 56F, ECOG 1, BRAF V600E confirmed by tissue NGS (PCR-orthogonal). Symptomatic CNS metastases with high LDH and rapid radiologic progression — visceral-crisis equivalent driving 1L BRAFi+MEKi over IO-doublet per DREAMseq. Engine routes ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L step 0 (KIT-mut absent) → step 1 (RF-MELANOMA-BRAF-V600-ACTIONABLE fires on braf_v600e_mutation=true) → step 2 (RF-MELANOMA-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION fires on symptomatic_cns_metastases=true) → IND-MELANOMA-BRAF-METASTATIC-1L-DABRA-TRAME (dabrafenib+trametinib, COMBI-d/v mPFS ~12 mo). Aggressive plan-track. NCCN cat 1.

Synthetic Stage IV cutaneous melanoma, 48M, ECOG 0, BRAF V600E confirmed by tissue NGS. Low-volume disease (oligometastatic lung), normal LDH, no CNS involvement, no visceral crisis. Per DREAMseq sequencing data, 1L IO-doublet preferred over BRAFi+MEKi for BRAF V600+ patients without symptomatic / rapidly-progressive disease. Engine routes ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L step 0 (KIT absent) → step 1 (RF-MELANOMA-BRAF-V600-ACTIONABLE fires) → step 2 (RF-MELANOMA-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION absent — symptomatic_cns_metastases=false) → IND-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L-NIVO-IPI (CheckMate-067 7.5y OS 49%). Standard plan-track. BRAFi+MEKi held in reserve for 2L per DREAMseq (post-IO ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-2L step 3).

Synthetic Stage IV cutaneous melanoma, 72F, ECOG 1, BRAF/NRAS/KIT wild-type by NGS. Patient has well-controlled autoimmune Hashimoto thyroiditis on stable levothyroxine + age 72 — relative contraindication to ipi+nivo Grade 3-4 irAE risk (~50%). Anchor target: KEYNOTE-006 pembrolizumab monotherapy. KB DRIFT NOTE: ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L has only two output indications (IND-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L-NIVO-IPI default + IND-MELANOMA-BRAF-METASTATIC-1L-DABRA-TRAME aggressive). There is NO 1L pembrolizumab-monotherapy indication in the metastatic-1L algorithm at this baseline (gap explicitly listed in algo_melanoma_metastatic_2l notes wiring-gap #2). Engine routes ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L step 0 (KIT absent) → step 1 (BRAF-V600-ACTIONABLE absent — branch result IND-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L-NIVO-IPI). Per intent the case still surfaces nivo+ipi as the standard-track default; an MDT note is appropriate to flag pembro-mono as an off-algorithm de-escalation for the irAE-frail subset (NCCN cat 1 KEYNOTE-006). Aggressive-track BRAFi+MEKi correctly suppressed since BRAF-WT.

Synthetic Stage IV cutaneous melanoma, 82F, ECOG 2, BRAF V600E confirmed by NGS. 1L received dabrafenib+trametinib (visceral-crisis biology) with progression at 11 mo — IO-naive at 2L. Age 82 + ECOG 2 + baseline limited-cutaneous psoriasis make ipi+nivo Grade 3-4 irAE risk (~50%) prohibitive. KB DRIFT NOTE: plan anchor was '1L nivolumab+relatlimab fixed-dose (RELATIVITY-047)' but at this KB baseline the only nivo+rela indication is IND-MELANOMA-2L-RELATLIMAB-NIVOLUMAB scoped to line_of_therapy=2 IO-naive post-BRAFi (RELATIVITY-047 was 1L per FDA; KB positions it as 2L IO-alternative for ipi-frail patients, off-label per indication notes). Case modeled at 2L accordingly. Engine routes ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-2L step 1 (KIT absent) → step 2 (RF-MELANOMA-IO-RESISTANT absent — IO-naive) → step 4 (RF-MELANOMA-BRAF-V600-ACTIONABLE fires) → step 41 (free-text ipi+nivo unfit — falls through) → step 5 (RF-MELANOMA-FRAILTY-AGE fires age≥80 + ECOG≥2) → IND-MELANOMA-2L-RELATLIMAB-NIVOLUMAB. UA access caveat: relatlimab unregistered — named-patient import pathway required.

label_en: Melanoma · Stage III resected · Adjuvant pembro (KEYNOTE-054)

json.comment: Synthetic Stage III resected cutaneous melanoma, 54M, ECOG 0, primary 3.2 mm Breslow with ulceration on the upper back, sentinel lymph node biopsy positive (1/3 nodes, single 2 mm metastasis), completed wide local excision + completion lymph-node dissection per surgeon judgment. BRAF V600E negative by NGS. Anchor target: KEYNOTE-054 adjuvant pembrolizumab × 12 months (12-mo recurrence-free benefit HR 0.57). KB DRIFT NOTE: at this KB baseline the melanoma KB has no adjuvant Stage III indication or algorithm — only ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L and ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-2L exist (gap explicitly noted in melanoma.yaml stage_strata stage_iii_regional vs the 1L/2L algorithms which are stage_iv only). Engine still resolves to ALGO-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L (the only line=1 melanoma algorithm) → step 0 (KIT absent) → step 1 (BRAF-V600-ACTIONABLE absent) → IND-MELANOMA-METASTATIC-1L-NIVO-IPI default. The metastatic-1L recommendation surfaced is NOT clinically appropriate for this resected Stage III patient — case carried for routing observability + to motivate a future ALGO-MELANOMA-ADJUVANT + IND-MELANOMA-ADJUVANT-PEMBRO-STAGE-III addition (KEYNOTE-054 adjuvant pembro × 12 mo). Engine smoke gates pass. MDT review required to override engine output until adjuvant track is wired.

Aggressive track surfaces as default; standard ARPI-switch track presented as alternative.

Synthetic clear-cell metastatic RCC, IMDC intermediate-risk male 62 (CheckMate-214 candidate for nivo+ipi). Plan target IND-RCC-METASTATIC-1L-NIVO-IPI. Drift acknowledged — RF authoring (RF-RCC-IMDC-INTERMEDIATE-POOR) tracked separately.

Synthetic clear-cell metastatic RCC, IMDC favorable-risk female 58 (KEYNOTE-426 candidate for axi+pembro). Engine routes ALGO-RCC-METASTATIC-1L → default IND-RCC-METASTATIC-1L-PEMBRO-AXI (favorable IMDC, no intermediate/poor RF triggered). KEYNOTE-426 mOS ~46 mo across IMDC risk groups.

Synthetic metastatic urothelial carcinoma (bladder primary), male 70, ECOG 1, no severe diabetes/neuropathy/ocular contraindications to enfortumab vedotin. Plan target IND-UROTHELIAL-METASTATIC-1L-EV-PEMBRO (EV-302: mOS 31.5 vs 16.1 mo platinum doublet, 2023). Drift: ALGO-UROTHELIAL-METASTATIC-1L step 1 uses free-text 'Patient eligible for EV+pembro' which has no matching disease-scoped RF, so the if_false branch fires and engine routes to IND-UROTHELIAL-METASTATIC-1L-PLATINUM-CHEMO-AVELUMAB (cisplatin+gem then avelumab maintenance). Drift acknowledged — RF authoring (RF-UROTHELIAL-EV-PEMBRO-ELIGIBLE / RF-UROTHELIAL-EV-CONTRAINDICATED) tracked separately. EV-PEMBRO is presented as alternative track for HCP consideration.

Synthetic advanced/recurrent endometrial endometrioid carcinoma, dMMR (MLH1/PMS2 IHC loss), female 64. Plan target IND-ENDOMETRIAL-ADVANCED-1L-PEMBRO-CHEMO (NRG-GY018, pembro+carbo+pacli). Engine routes ALGO-ENDOMETRIAL-ADVANCED-1L → default IND-ENDOMETRIAL-ADVANCED-1L-PEMBRO-CHEMO (no drift; algorithm step-1 'dMMR / MSI-high tumor' is free-text without disease-scoped RF, so default applies and KN-775/NRG-GY018 supports pembro+chemo across MMR strata). Lynch-syndrome germline workup triggered by dMMR finding.

Synthetic advanced/recurrent endometrial serous carcinoma, p53-abnormal (TCGA copy-number-high subtype), pMMR, female 68. Plan target IND-ENDOMETRIAL-ADVANCED-1L-DOSTARLIMAB-CHEMO (RUBY: dostarlimab+carbo+pacli). Drift: ALGO-ENDOMETRIAL-ADVANCED-1L step 1 uses free-text 'dMMR / MSI-high tumor' as the dostarlimab branch trigger, so engine routes pMMR p53-abn cases to default IND-ENDOMETRIAL-ADVANCED-1L-PEMBRO-CHEMO. Drift acknowledged — RF authoring (RF-ENDOMETRIAL-DMMR / molecular-subtype routing) and broader p53-abn / RUBY-Part-2-style routing tracked separately.

label_en: Ovarian · HRD-neg · Carbo+pacli, no PARPi maintenance

json.comment: Synthetic high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma FIGO IIIC, HRD-negative (BRCA1/2 wild-type, GIS<42), female 62. Engine walks ALGO-OVARIAN-ADVANCED-1L: step 3 (BRCA-mut RF) false → step 4 (HRD-positive RFs) false → step 5 (frailty RF) false → step 6 default → IND-OVARIAN-ADVANCED-1L-CARBO-PACLI-HRD-NEG. Carbo+pacli induction × 6, NO olaparib maintenance (HRD-neg subset has minimal benefit + MDS/AML risk). Bev maintenance per ICON-7/GOG-218 optional. Contrast case to existing patient_ovarian_advanced_hrd.json.

Synthetic advanced HCC, BCLC C, Child-Pugh A5, MASH cirrhosis, no varices on recent EGD, ECOG 1, AFP 620 ng/mL, HBV/HCV/HIV negative. Engine routes ALGO-HCC-SYSTEMIC-1L step 1 (no variceal bleed) → step 2 (no CP-B/C, no frailty) → step 3 (no hepatic dysfunction RF + RF-FITNESS-ECOG-FIT fires) → IND-HCC-SYSTEMIC-1L-ATEZO-BEV (atezolizumab+bevacizumab, IMbrave150 mOS 19.2 vs 13.4 sorafenib, NCCN cat 1).

Synthetic advanced HCC, BCLC C, Child-Pugh A6, HCV-cirrhosis (post-SVR12), ECOG 1, AFP 180 ng/mL, prior variceal bleed 4 months ago (banded, controlled) — bevacizumab contraindicated. Synthetic limited-stage SCLC, female 62, heavy ex-smoker, ECOG 1, disease confined to right hemithorax encompassable in single tolerable RT field, no distant metastases, brain MRI clear. CLINICAL TARGET: IND-SCLC-LIMITED-1L (REG-EP-CONCURRENT-RT-SCLC-LIMITED, CONVERT 60 Gy once-daily, mOS ~27 mo). KB-DRIFT NOTE 1: ALGO-SCLC-1L step 1 evaluates a free-text 'Limited-stage' condition with no matching disease-scoped RedFlag; the algorithm itself flags this in its notes ('Flag for RF authoring'). Engine therefore cannot detect limited stage from findings and falls through to default IND-SCLC-EXTENSIVE-1L (durvalumab/CASPIAN); IND-SCLC-LIMITED-1L surfaces as alternative-track for HCP consideration. KB-DRIFT NOTE 2 (CHARTER §17): radiotherapy is not modellable in current schema — REG-EP-CONCURRENT-RT-SCLC-LIMITED references concurrent thoracic RT in its name/notes but RT itself is not a structured Drug component, so the rendered Plan surfaces only the chemo backbone (etoposide+platinum). The case is published to make both gaps visible to KB authors and tumor-board reviewers.

Synthetic extensive-stage SCLC, male 67, heavy current-smoker, ECOG 1, disease beyond one hemithorax with bilateral lung + liver + bone metastases, brain MRI clear. CLINICAL TARGET: IMpower133 archetype — REG-EP-ATEZO-SCLC (carboplatin AUC5 + etoposide x4 + atezolizumab maintenance, mOS 12.3 mo per IMpower133 Horn NEJM 2018). KB-DRIFT NOTE: KB contains BOTH REG-EP-ATEZO-SCLC (IMpower133) and REG-EP-DURVA-SCLC (CASPIAN), but IND-SCLC-EXTENSIVE-1L recommends only REG-EP-DURVA-SCLC. Per ind notes: 'NCCN considers EP+atezo (IMpower133) and EP+durva (CASPIAN) equivalent. Choice institutional.' Engine will route to IND-SCLC-EXTENSIVE-1L (default) and render the durvalumab regimen — the IMpower133 framing in patient_id/comment is the intended clinical archetype but the rendered Plan will print durvalumab. Tumor board may substitute atezolizumab per institutional preference using the existing REG-EP-ATEZO-SCLC entity. Flagged to KB authors as a candidate for a parallel IND-SCLC-EXTENSIVE-1L-IMPOWER variant.

Synthetic recurrent / metastatic HNSCC, male 64, oropharyngeal SCC, HPV-negative (p16 IHC negative), heavy ex-smoker + alcohol, ECOG 1, recurrence at 14 months post-definitive cisplatin-RT, now with new lung metastases. PD-L1 CPS 35 by 22C3 IHC, no rapid progression / visceral crisis. CLINICAL TARGET: IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-PEMBRO-MONO-CPS-HIGH (KEYNOTE-048 Burtness Lancet 2019, mOS 14.9 mo, ~35% 24-mo OS, mDOR ~22 mo). ENGINE ROUTING: ALGO-HNSCC-RM-1L step 1 fires RF-HNSCC-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION because the new_distant_metastasis trigger is satisfied (any_of clause does not require concurrent locoregional recurrence). Engine therefore selects IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-PEMBRO-MONO-CPS-HIGH as default (matching clinical target by route, even though the upstream trigger semantic is broader than the algorithm's free-text 'CPS ≥20' description suggests). KB-DRIFT NOTE: the algorithm's step-1 condition ('PD-L1 CPS ≥20 AND no rapid progression AND ECOG 0-2') is free-text and cannot be evaluated — the structured RF that drives the branch is RF-HNSCC-TRANSFORMATION-PROGRESSION, which fires on ANY new distant metastasis regardless of CPS. A patient with CPS 0 + new mets would also be routed to pembro-mono today; that is incorrect clinically. RF authoring gap flagged: a CPS-stratifying RedFlag is needed for HNSCC R/M 1L, and the transformation-progression RF should be tightened (require co-occurring locoregional / airway / carotid signal, not standalone new-mets).

Synthetic recurrent / metastatic HNSCC, male 59, hypopharyngeal SCC, HPV-negative, current heavy smoker + heavy alcohol use, ECOG 1, lung + bone metastases at presentation, PD-L1 CPS 0 (negative). CLINICAL TARGET: EXTREME regimen archetype — cetuximab + cisplatin + 5-FU per Vermorken NEJM 2008 (mOS 10.1 mo vs 7.4 mo platin+5FU alone). KB-DRIFT NOTE (most prominent of the chunk): no IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-EXTREME exists in the KB. ALGO-HNSCC-RM-1L explicitly notes: 'CPS <1 R/M HNSCC ideally routes to EXTREME (cetuximab + 5-FU + platinum) or chemo-IO with attenuated OS benefit; explicit IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-EXTREME is deferred to a follow-up content session.' Engine therefore returns default IND-HNSCC-RM-1L-PEMBRO-CHEMO regardless of CPS<1 — the CPS-stratified branch is not authored, and chemo-IO with attenuated benefit is the surrogate the engine prints. The IND IS contraindicated for this CPS<1 profile per evidence (HR for OS in CPS<1 subset of KEYNOTE-048 chemo-IO arm crossed 1.0). Tumor board must be aware that the rendered Plan is the closest-available KB recommendation, NOT the actual standard for CPS<1 — clinician judgment required to substitute EXTREME or another cetuximab-based platinum doublet. Flagged for KB authors as the highest-priority missing Indication in HNSCC.

json.comment: Synthetic 3L axi-cel candidate. Distinct from existing patient_dlbcl_chemorefractory_for_cart.json (which is pre-decision CAR-T candidate from 1L R-CHOP → 2L R-ICE / chemorefractory archetype). This case is the post-decision continuation of the H2 line-of-therapy chain: 1L R-CHOP CR → late relapse → 2L Pola-BR → progression at 7 mo → 3L axi-cel administration plan. Age 56 fit ECOG 1, CD19+ confirmed, no CNS disease. Engine routes ALGO-DLBCL-2L: standard-track DEFAULT = IND-DLBCL-2L-POLA-R-BENDAMUSTINE (step 3 false branch fallback), aggressive-track ALTERNATIVE = IND-DLBCL-3L-AXICEL-CART (ZUMA-1; ORR ~83%, CR ~58%, 5y OS ~43%). The 3L axi-cel intent surfaces correctly as the aggressive-track curative-intent option presented to the MDT. KB-DRIFT NOTE: this case sets line_of_therapy=2 because ALGO-DLBCL-2L applicable_to_line_of_therapy=2, even though clinically this is 3L (post 2 prior lines); existing chemorefractory_for_cart.json sets line_of_therapy=3 and consequently fails algorithm match at baseline (exit=1, Algorithm: None) — there is no ALGO-DLBCL-3L in KB. Surfacing this drift: KB should add ALGO-DLBCL-3L or generalize line_of_therapy matching to ≥. Secondary drift: ALGO-DLBCL-2L step 1 references RF-ASCT-ELIGIBLE-COMPOSITE which doesn't fully model post-decline state, so step 2 axi-cel branch never fires deterministically as default (gated on multiple findings + RF wiring gaps noted in algo notes).

Age 71, ECOG 2, frail, multiple grade-2 comorbidities; not transplant-eligible AND not CAR-T-eligible (no UA international referral pathway). Clinically the textbook indication is loncastuximab tesirine 3L+ (LOTIS-2 Caimi 2021 Lancet Oncol; ORR 48%, CR 24%, mPFS 4.9 mo, mOS 9.5 mo) — off-the-shelf outpatient ADC ideally suited for non-CAR-T-eligible frail patient. KB-DRIFT NOTE: engine routes ALGO-DLBCL-2L (LOT=2 set per algorithm match constraint; clinically post-2L) → standard-track DEFAULT = IND-DLBCL-2L-POLA-R-BENDAMUSTINE (step 3 fallback), aggressive-track ALTERNATIVE = IND-DLBCL-3L-AXICEL-CART. The intended IND-DLBCL-3L-LONCASTUXIMAB is NOT surfaced because (a) it is not in ALGO-DLBCL-2L.output_indications list, (b) its YAML has empty recommended_regimen (no REG-LONCASTUXIMAB-TESIRINE entity yet — flagged STUB), and (c) no ALGO-DLBCL-3L exists. Surfacing real KB drift: loncastuximab pathway needs (i) REG-LONCASTUXIMAB-TESIRINE authoring, (ii) inclusion in ALGO-DLBCL-2L step branches gated on CAR-T-ineligible composite, (iii) and/or new ALGO-DLBCL-3L for frail post-2L cohort. Default Pola-BR is clinically inappropriate for this patient (already failed Pola-based 2L Pola-R-CHP — re-treating with same MMAE class is futile + neuropathy risk Grade 1 already present).

Suspicion of acute leukemia — possibly APL given coagulopathy. No prior cytotoxic therapy, no antecedent MDS documented. ED started TLS prophylaxis (IV fluids, allopurinol). Hyperleukocytosis_with_blasts present.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect CRC · Iron-deficiency + LGIB + colon mass
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect CRC · Iron-deficiency + LGIB + colon mass

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 58F with iron-deficiency anemia (Hgb 8.4, ferritin 9) + intermittent rectal bleeding × 4 mo + 6 kg weight loss. CT abdomen shows 4.5 cm right-colon mass, no obvious mets but liver lesions <1cm need MRI. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-CRC and emit Diagnostic Brief — colonoscopy + biopsy + staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis + CEA + KRAS/NRAS/BRAF + MSI/MMR-IHC required before Tx selection.

suspicion.presentation: 58F PCP-referred for iron-deficiency anemia (Hgb 8.4 g/dL, ferritin 9 ng/mL, TSat 6%) discovered on annual labs. ED visit 3 months ago for melena_with_iron_deficiency, treated with PPI + iron. Returned with intermittent rectal_bleeding × 4 months, change_in_bowel_habits (alternating diarrhea/constipation), 6 kg unintentional weight_loss. CT abd/pelvis (without contrast — CrCl 38): 4.5 cm cecal/right-colon wall_thickening with mesenteric LAD. Two sub-cm liver hypodensities — characterization needs MRI. No prior CRC screening. FOBT positive. Strong suspicion CRC.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect endometrial · PMB + thickened endometrium 14 mm
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect endometrial · PMB + thickened endometrium 14 mm

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 64F postmenopausal x 12 yrs with new-onset PMB × 6 weeks + thickened endometrium 14 mm on TVUS. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-ENDOMETRIAL and emit Diagnostic Brief — endometrial biopsy (Pipelle) ± hysteroscopy required for histology + grade + molecular (POLE / dMMR / p53 / NSMP TCGA stratification) before staging/Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 64F postmenopausal × 12 years (LMP age 52) presents with new-onset postmenopausal_bleeding × 6 weeks, intermittent spotting → frank bleeding past 2 weeks. Denies pelvic pain, abdominal mass, weight change. TVUS shows endometrial_stripe 14 mm (PMB threshold >5 mm), heterogeneous, no mass-like distortion. CA-125 normal. PAP smear last age 60 (negative). BMI 34, T2DM × 8 yrs (Lynch / type-I endometrial risk profile). No HRT.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect GBM · 4.2 cm ring-enhancing left frontal mass
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect GBM · 4.2 cm ring-enhancing left frontal mass

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 62M with new-onset right-hand weakness + word-finding difficulty × 3 weeks; brain MRI shows 4.2 cm ring-enhancing left frontal mass with central necrosis + vasogenic edema. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-GBM and emit Diagnostic Brief — neurosurgical biopsy/resection required for histology + IDH-1/2 + MGMT methylation + 1p/19q before treatment.

suspicion.presentation: 62M presents to neurology with progressive right-hand weakness + intermittent expressive aphasia (word-finding difficulty) × 3 weeks. No headache initially; recent onset mild morning headache + 2 episodes of focal motor seizure (right-hand twitching, no LOC). Brain_MRI_with_contrast: 4.2 cm left frontal mass with ring-enhancement, central necrosis, surrounding T2 vasogenic_edema, midline shift 4 mm. Left precentral cortex involvement. No leptomeningeal enhancement. Started dexamethasone 4 mg q6h + levetiracetam 500 mg BID. Imaging strongly suspicious for glioblastoma vs metastatic disease — biopsy required.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect HCC · HCV-cirrhosis + LI-RADS 5 + AFP 480
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect HCC · HCV-cirrhosis + LI-RADS 5 + AFP 480

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 59M with HCV-cirrhosis (post-DAA SVR12 4 yrs ago) + 3.8 cm liver mass on surveillance US, AFP 480 ng/mL. Multi-phase CT shows LI-RADS 5 lesion (arterial enhancement + washout). Per AASLD HCC may be diagnosed by imaging alone in cirrhotic patient with LI-RADS 5 — but biopsy required if molecular markers needed (rare) OR diagnosis ambiguous. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-HCC and emit Diagnostic Brief — Child-Pugh classification + BCLC staging + variceal screening + biopsy decision before Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 59M HCV-cirrhosis (Child-Pugh A6, MELD 9) post-DAA sofosbuvir/velpatasvir 12 weeks → SVR12 confirmed 4 years ago. On surveillance US-q6mo + AFP. New 3.8 cm hepatic mass detected on routine US (right hepatic lobe segment 7), AFP rose 12 → 480 ng/mL over 6 months. Multi-phase contrast CT: arterial_phase_hyperenhancement + portal_venous_washout + capsular appearance → LI-RADS_5 (definitive HCC by imaging in cirrhotic patient per AASLD criteria). No portal vein invasion, no extrahepatic disease. Esophageal varices grade 1 on prior EGD (2 years ago — needs re-screening pre-systemic Tx). Eligible for systemic therapy or curative bridging (TACE/transplant) per BCLC.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect melanoma · Atypical pigmented lesion (ABCDE+)
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect melanoma · Atypical pigmented lesion (ABCDE+)

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 51M with newly noticed 1.4 cm pigmented dorsal-back lesion + ABCDE criteria (asymmetry, irregular border, color variation, diameter >6mm, evolving) + history of significant UV exposure. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-MELANOMA and emit Diagnostic Brief — excisional biopsy with 1-3 mm margins required before staging (sentinel LN biopsy if Breslow ≥0.8 mm or ulcerated) + BRAF V600 testing for therapy selection.

suspicion.presentation: 51M software engineer presents to dermatology after wife noticed pigmented_skin_lesion on his upper back during beach vacation. He reports the changing_nevus has evolved over 9 months — was 'a small mole' he had since teenage years, now new_pigmented_growth ~1.4 cm with darker center and asymmetric border. abcde_lesion positive on all five criteria. Significant lifetime UV exposure (estimated 200+ days outdoor sailing/skiing without sunscreen during 20s-30s). No prior skin cancers; family history negative for melanoma. Dermoscopy shows atypical pigment network + atypical dots + structureless areas. No palpable regional lymphadenopathy. Strongly suspicious for cutaneous_melanoma_suspect — excisional biopsy with 2 mm margins planned.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect MM · Bone pain + IgG-kappa M-spike + lytic L2
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect MM · Bone pain + IgG-kappa M-spike + lytic L2

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 65M with new-onset thoracic back pain + lytic L2 lesion on CT + IgG-kappa monoclonal protein 32 g/L on SPEP. Renal function impaired (CrCl 38). Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-MULTIPLE-MYELOMA and emit Diagnostic Brief — BM aspirate (≥10% plasma cells) + skeletal survey/whole-body MRI + serum FLC ratio + cytogenetics FISH (t(4;14)/t(14;16)/del(17p)) for risk stratification before Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 65M presents to PCP with progressive thoracic back pain × 8 weeks, refractory to NSAIDs + acetaminophen. Pain worse at night, with movement. No trauma. Fatigue + 4 kg unintentional weight loss + nocturia × 2-3/night. PCP labs: Hgb 10.1, Cr 1.6 (CrCl 38, baseline 0.9 6 mo ago — new RI), corrected Ca 11.8 (mild hyperCa), total protein 9.2 with low albumin (4.1) → elevated_globulin_gap. SPEP: IgG_kappa monoclonal_protein 32 g/L (M-spike). Serum free light chains: kappa 280 mg/L, lambda 18 mg/L (κ:λ ratio 15.6, abnormal). β2-microglobulin 6.8 mg/L. Urine: light chain 3+ (proteinuria_with_low_albumin). CT thoracic spine: lytic_bone_lesion at L2 (1.8 cm). C/M/A/B SLiM-CRAB criteria met (anemia + hyperCa + RI + lytic lesion). High suspicion of multiple myeloma — needs BM biopsy for plasma-cell percentage + cytogenetics.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect MDS · Pancytopenia + dysplastic changes + JAK2-neg
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect MDS · Pancytopenia + dysplastic changes + JAK2-neg

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 71F with progressive pancytopenia × 4 months + macrocytosis + mild splenomegaly. Reticulocyte index low. Smear shows dysplastic neutrophils + Pelger-Huet anomaly. Suspicion of MDS vs MPN-overlap (CMML) vs aplastic anemia. Engine matches WORKUP-CYTOPENIA-EVALUATION (the broader pancytopenia workup which is correct given chief complaint — MPN-MDS-specific workup expects erythrocytosis/thrombocytosis/leukocytosis keywords for proliferative MPN, not cytopenic MDS). Emits Diagnostic Brief — BM aspirate + cytogenetics + IPSS-R/IPSS-M + JAK2/CALR/MPL/SF3B1/ASXL1/TET2 NGS panel before Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 71F retired teacher referred for evaluation of progressive pancytopenia × 4 months (per serial CBCs at PCP). Hgb declined 13.2 → 9.4 over 4 months (now transfusion-naïve), MCV 108 (macrocytic), platelets 92, ANC 1.1. No B-vitamin / folate deficiency (B12 normal, folate normal). Erythropoietin level 380 mU/mL (elevated, suggesting non-renal cause). Reticulocyte index 0.4 (inappropriately low for anemia). Peripheral smear: 5% dysplastic_neutrophils with hypogranulation, occasional Pelger-Huet anomaly, no circulating blasts. Splenomegaly 14 cm on US (mild). No B-symptoms, no LAD. JAK2-V617F negative on send-out (preliminary). Suspicion: MDS (lower-risk) vs MPN-overlap (CMML if monocyte criteria met).

label: Diagnostic · Suspect PV · Erythrocytosis (Hgb 19.2) + low EPO + aquagenic pruritus
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect PV · Erythrocytosis (Hgb 19.2) + low EPO + aquagenic pruritus

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 58M with new-onset facial plethora + aquagenic pruritus + erythrocytosis (Hgb 19.2 g/dL) + leukocytosis + mild thrombocytosis + splenomegaly. Suspicion of polycythemia vera vs secondary erythrocytosis. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-MPN-MDS and emit Diagnostic Brief — JAK2 V617F + JAK2 exon 12 mutation testing + serum erythropoietin + BM biopsy required before WHO 2022 PV diagnosis confirmation and risk-stratified Tx (low-dose ASA + phlebotomy ± hydroxyurea).

suspicion.presentation: 58M presents with 6-month history of aquagenic pruritus (intense itching after warm shower), facial plethora, occasional headache, blurred vision, and one episode of digital ischemia (transient erythromelalgia) of right index finger. CBC over 3 visits: Hgb 19.2 g/dL (markedly elevated, baseline 14.8 two years prior), Hct 58%, WBC 13.4 (leukocytosis), platelets 562 (mild thrombocytosis), erythrocytosis confirmed. Splenomegaly 16 cm on US. Serum erythropoietin LOW 2.1 mU/mL (suppressed — appropriate for primary erythrocytosis vs elevated in secondary). LDH mildly elevated 245. No tobacco / no chronic hypoxic disease / no testosterone supplementation / no high-altitude exposure. JAK2 V617F testing pending. Strong suspicion of polycythemia vera per WHO 2022 major criteria 1 (Hgb >16.5 M / >16 F) + minor low EPO.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect ovarian · Bilateral adnexal masses + CA-125 412 + ascites
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect ovarian · Bilateral adnexal masses + CA-125 412 + ascites

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 58F with bloating + early-satiety × 2 mo + bilateral complex adnexal masses 8.5 cm/4.2 cm on TVUS + CA-125 412. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-OVARIAN and emit Diagnostic Brief — primary cytoreductive surgery for staging + histology (high-grade serous most likely) + HRD + BRCA1/2 germline testing required before adjuvant Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 58F postmenopausal × 6 yrs presents with insidious abdominal_bloating × 2 months + early_satiety + 4 kg unintentional weight loss + new mild ascites on exam. PCP suspected GI etiology initially (referred for upper endoscopy → normal). TVUS: bilateral complex_adnexal_masses — right 8.5 × 6.8 cm with mural nodules + papillary projections + septations + some solid components (Sassone score elevated; IOTA O-RADS 5), left 4.2 cm similar morphology. Mild ascites. CA-125 412 U/mL (markedly elevated, normal <35). HE4 elevated. ROMA index high-risk. CEA + CA-19-9 normal. CT abd/pelvis: omental thickening + peritoneal implants ≤2 cm, no liver mets, no pleural effusion. No prior gynecologic malignancy. BRCA testing not yet performed — important for surgery planning.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect RCC · 5.4 cm incidental enhancing renal mass
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect RCC · 5.4 cm incidental enhancing renal mass

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 64M with 5.4 cm enhancing right renal mass found incidentally on CT for chronic LBP. RENAL nephrometry score 9 (intermediate). Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-RCC and emit Diagnostic Brief — staging CT chest/abd/pelvis + bone scan if symptomatic + radical/partial nephrectomy + histology (clear cell vs papillary vs chromophobe) before adjuvant Tx (sunitinib/pembro per S-TRAC/KEYNOTE-564).

suspicion.presentation: 64M presents to spine clinic for chronic non-radicular low_back_pain × 6 months. Lumbar MRI without contrast was unrevealing (mild facet arthropathy). Subsequent CT abd/pelvis with contrast (added per radiologist suggestion to rule out occult cause) revealed incidental_renal_mass — 5.4 cm contrast-enhancing right_kidney upper-pole lesion, exophytic component, possibly involves renal sinus fat (need MRI for staging). RENAL nephrometry score 9 (intermediate complexity). No regional LAD, no IVC tumor thrombus on CT, no liver/lung mets seen. CBC + CMP normal except mild microscopic hematuria on UA. No flank pain, no hematuria, no constitutional symptoms — classic incidental RCC presentation. eGFR 76 (preserved). Smoker × 30 pack-years (recent quit 2 years ago). Suspicion strongly favors RCC.

label: Diagnostic · Suspect SCLC · Central 5.8 cm mass + paraneoplastic SIADH (Na 124)
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect SCLC · Central 5.8 cm mass + paraneoplastic SIADH (Na 124)

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 71M heavy smoker with 5.8 cm central lung mass + paraneoplastic SIADH (Na 124) + bulky mediastinal LAD. Chest CT pattern strongly suspicious for SCLC (vs central NSCLC). Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-SCLC and emit Diagnostic Brief — bronchoscopic biopsy + EBUS-LN sampling for histology + brain MRI for occult mets + LDH staging (SCLC has limited-stage vs extensive-stage stratification) before Tx (atezo+chemo IMpower133 vs PCI consideration).

suspicion.presentation: 71M heavy smoker (62 pack-years, current) presents with 6-week progressive cough + 4 kg weight loss + new-onset confusion + fatigue. Labs: Na 124 mEq/L (hyponatremia, baseline 138 6 months ago). Serum osmolality 268, urine osmolality 580, urine Na 65 → SIADH pattern. CXR: 5.8 cm right_perihilar_mass with bulky mediastinal LAD. CT chest: central_lung_mass invading right main bronchus + mediastinal LAD (4R + 7 stations) + bilateral hilar LAD + small right pleural effusion. No bone/liver mets on CT (need PET-CT). LDH 612 (elevated). Imaging + paraneoplastic_siadh in heavy smoker strongly suspicious for small_cell_lung_cancer (SCLC has higher SIADH association than NSCLC).

label: Diagnostic · Suspect urothelial · Gross painless hematuria + 2.8 cm bladder mass
label_en: Diagnostic · Suspect urothelial · Gross painless hematuria + 2.8 cm bladder mass

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 68M heavy smoker with 3 episodes of gross painless hematuria + cystoscopy showing 2.8 cm bladder mass on right wall. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-UROTHELIAL and emit Diagnostic Brief — TURBT (transurethral resection) for histology + muscle invasion assessment + FGFR3/PDL1/HER2 panel before staging Tx (NMIBC intravesical BCG vs MIBC neoadjuvant cisplatin chemo + cystectomy vs metastatic systemic).

suspicion.presentation: 68M heavy smoker (45 pack-years, current) presents to urology with 3 episodes of gross_painless_hematuria over 6 weeks (visible blood in urine, no clots, no dysuria, no flank pain). PCP UA showed 50+ RBC/hpf, no infection. Urine cytology atypical urothelial cells, suspicious for malignancy. CT urogram: 2.8 cm right_lateral_wall_bladder_mass with no obvious wall invasion on CT (need TURBT for muscle invasion assessment). No upper tract lesions. No regional LAD. Cystoscopy in clinic confirmed papillary mass with sessile component. Patient counseled for TURBT + bimanual EUA to assess for cT2+ disease. No prior bladder cancer, no occupational chemical exposure.

label: Diagnostic · Persistent LAD undifferentiated · Reactive vs lymphoma vs metastatic
label_en: Diagnostic · Persistent LAD undifferentiated · Reactive vs lymphoma vs metastatic

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 35F with bilateral cervical + right axillary LAD × 7 weeks, no B-symptoms, no infectious exposures. Reactive vs lymphoma vs metastatic still ambiguous. Engine should match WORKUP-LYMPHADENOPATHY-NONSPECIFIC (triage workup before lymphoma-specific path) and emit Diagnostic Brief — CBC + CMP + LDH + ESR/CRP + viral serologies + excisional biopsy of largest accessible node before specific Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 35F nurse presents with persistent palpable_lymph_node bilateral cervical chains (left 1.8 cm, right 2.2 cm) + right axillary node 1.5 cm × 7 weeks. No tenderness. No B-symptoms (no fevers, night sweats <1 episode/wk only, weight stable). No recent infections, no animal/tick exposures, no foreign travel. CBC normal, ESR mildly elevated 38, LDH normal. Mono spot negative, EBV / CMV serologies pending. CT neck/chest/abd: bilateral cervical + right axillary lymphadenopathy + small mediastinal nodes 8-10 mm. No splenomegaly. No primary malignancy identified on imaging. Lymphadenitis vs reactive vs early lymphoma vs metastatic carcinoma — needs excisional biopsy of largest cervical node for histology before further workup.

label: Diagnostic · mPC restage · Rising PSA + bone mets post-RP (Gleason 9)
label_en: Diagnostic · mPC restage · Rising PSA + bone mets post-RP (Gleason 9)

json._comment: Workup profile: 73M with biopsy-confirmed prostate adeno (Gleason 4+5=9) on radical prostatectomy 4 yrs ago, now with rising PSA (current 12.4, doubling time 4 mo) + new bone pain + suspected mets on bone scan. Engine should match WORKUP-METASTATIC-PROSTATE (post-confirmed-disease metastatic-staging workup) and emit Diagnostic Brief — full staging restage with PSMA-PET + germline BRCA panel + somatic HRR panel + testosterone confirmation before mHSPC vs mCRPC Tx selection.

suspicion.presentation: 73M with prostate_cancer_metastatic suspect — biopsy-confirmed prostate adeno (Gleason 4+5=9, pT3a, ISUP 5) on radical prostatectomy 4 yrs ago, post-op PSA nadir 0.05. Rising_psa over past 12 months: 0.05 → 0.8 → 4.2 → 12.4 ng/mL (PSA doubling time 4 months — rapid). New onset of right hip and lumbar back pain × 6 weeks. NM bone scan: increased uptake L3 vertebral body + right iliac wing → bone_metastases suspect. Pelvic MRI: prostate fossa unremarkable, but 2 new pelvic lymph nodes 1.4 + 1.1 cm. Testosterone 480 ng/dL (castrate-sensitive — mhspc vs early progression). Pre-existing hypertension + T2DM + CrCl 62. No prior systemic therapy (post-op surveillance only). Needs PSMA-PET for accurate staging + germline + somatic HRR before Tx (ARPI doublet vs triplet, PARPi if BRCA+).

label: Diagnostic · MGUS · Asymptomatic M-spike 8 g/L IgG-kappa (incidental)
label_en: Diagnostic · MGUS · Asymptomatic M-spike 8 g/L IgG-kappa (incidental)

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 71M asymptomatic with incidental M-spike (IgG-kappa 8 g/L) on routine SPEP done for hyperglobulinemia noted on annual labs. No CRAB criteria, no symptoms. Engine should match WORKUP-MONOCLONAL-GAMMOPATHY-INCIDENTAL (MGUS triage workup, not full MM workup) and emit Diagnostic Brief — IMWG 2014 criteria stratification (MGUS vs smoldering MM vs active MM), serum FLC ratio, urine immunofixation, BM biopsy if FLC ratio abnormal or M-spike >1.5 g/L, before risk-stratified surveillance vs Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 71M asymptomatic, comes for routine annual physical. PCP labs revealed serum_globulin_high 4.8 g/dL (total protein 8.4, albumin 3.6 — elevated globulin gap). Reflex SPEP confirmed monoclonal_protein 8 g/L IgG-kappa (m_spike_incidental finding). No CRAB criteria — Hgb 13.2, Cr 0.9 (CrCl 78), Ca 9.4, no skeletal symptoms, no lytic lesions on screening skeletal survey. No fatigue, no recurrent infections, no peripheral neuropathy, no hyperviscosity symptoms. Serum free light chains: kappa 24, lambda 18, ratio 1.33 (NORMAL <1.65). β2-microglobulin 2.1 (normal). Per IMWG 2014 criteria — likely low-risk MGUS (size <1.5 g/L, IgG type, normal FLC ratio = ZERO risk factors). Needs IMWG risk stratification + paraprotein_screen + BM biopsy decision (skip if low-risk + normal FLC ratio per current 2014 guidelines).

label: Diagnostic · Lung cancer suspect · LDCT screening + 3.2 cm RUL mass (NSCLC vs SCLC)
label_en: Diagnostic · Lung cancer suspect · LDCT screening + 3.2 cm RUL mass (NSCLC vs SCLC)

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 65M heavy smoker with 3.2 cm RUL lung mass + 2 hilar nodes on screening LDCT, NSCLC vs SCLC vs metastatic still unknown. Engine should match WORKUP-NSCLC-DIAGNOSIS (broader lung-cancer workup pre-histology, before NSCLC-specific NGS panel) and emit Diagnostic Brief — bronchoscopy/CT-guided biopsy + EBUS-LN sampling + cytology + IHC (TTF-1, CK7, CK5/6, p40 for NSCLC subtype + synaptophysin/chromogranin for neuroendocrine) + staging CT/PET-CT before NGS panel and Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 65M heavy smoker (40 pack-years, current) referred from screening LDCT program — 3.2 cm spiculated mass right upper lobe + 2 enlarged right hilar nodes (largest 1.4 cm). solitary_pulmonary_nodule on initial LDCT actually evolved into mass on diagnostic CT. Subsequent CT chest with contrast: irregular lung_mass with mediastinal LAD level 4R + 7. PET-CT showed FDG-avid primary + LN + small adrenal nodule (1.1 cm, equivocal). No bone uptake. No brain MRI yet. Persistent_cough × 4 months, mild hemoptysis × 2 episodes. Histology pending bronchoscopic biopsy (scheduled). NSCLC vs SCLC vs metastatic carcinoma differential — central location + smoker raises SCLC concern but no paraneoplastic syndromes. Workup at the post-imaging / pre-histology stage where NSCLC-vs-SCLC differentiation matters for Tx pathway selection.

label: Diagnostic · CUP · 4 liver mets + bone lesions, occult primary
label_en: Diagnostic · CUP · 4 liver mets + bone lesions, occult primary

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 67F with multiple liver metastases on imaging + bony lesions + slightly elevated CA 19-9 + CEA + adenocarcinoma on liver-mass biopsy IHC (CK7+/CK20-/TTF-1-/GATA3-) — pattern suggests upper-GI / pancreaticobiliary / lung-non-small primary, but no primary identified on PET-CT. Cancer of Unknown Primary (CUP). Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-SOLID-TUMOR (generic catch-all when site not yet specified) and emit Diagnostic Brief — extensive panel for primary identification + molecular profiling (NGS, MSI/MMR, BRAF, EGFR for CUP-favorable subsets) + tissue-of-origin classifier before site-directed Tx.

suspicion.presentation: 67F presents with 8-week fatigue + 6 kg weight loss + new mid-thoracic back pain. CT abd/pelvis (with contrast): multiple liver lesion x4, largest 4.2 cm, hypovascular pattern. CT chest: no lung mass, small mediastinal nodes <1 cm. Bone scan + skeletal MRI: lytic+sclerotic mixed lesions T6, T9, right iliac wing. CEA 24 (mildly elevated), CA 19-9 280 (elevated), CA 125 38 (mildly elevated), AFP normal. Liver-mass biopsy histology: poorly-differentiated adenocarcinoma. IHC pattern: CK7-positive, CK20-negative, TTF-1-negative, GATA3-negative, ER-negative, p40-negative. Pattern is non-specific upper-GI / pancreaticobiliary / lung-non-small primary, but no primary tumor seen on diagnostic imaging. PET-CT revealed only known liver+bone+nodal disease, no FDG-avid primary visible. CUP per ESMO criteria. Tumor-of-origin classifier (e.g., CancerType ID, methylation panel) + comprehensive NGS for actionable drivers needed.

label: Diagnostic · Breast post-biopsy staging · ER+/HER2-low + axillary node + family hx
label_en: Diagnostic · Breast post-biopsy staging · ER+/HER2-low + axillary node + family hx

json._comment: Post-imaging confirmed diagnostic profile: 47F with biopsy-confirmed left breast invasive ductal carcinoma (core biopsy ER+/PR+/HER2-low IHC 2+ FISH-) on BIRADS-5 imaging, ready for staging + receptor confirmation + germline + Oncotype-DX before final Tx. Distinct from WORKUP-SUSPECTED-BREAST (pre-biopsy lump) — this is the post-histology staging workup. Engine should match WORKUP-BREAST-DIAGNOSIS (post-biopsy staging path) and emit Diagnostic Brief — staging CT + receptor confirmation IHC + germline BRCA + Oncotype-DX + axillary US + sentinel-LN-biopsy planning before adjuvant Tx selection.

suspicion.presentation: 47F with biopsy-confirmed solid_tumor_breast — invasive ductal carcinoma G2 on left upper-outer quadrant core biopsy (BIRADS-5 mammography 2.6 cm spiculated mass + targeted core biopsy). IHC: ER 95%+, PR 70%+, HER2 IHC 2+ → reflex FISH HER2/CEP17 1.6 (NEGATIVE per ASCO/CAP 2023). Ki-67 18%. Strong family history (mother breast Ca age 51, maternal aunt ovarian Ca age 47) — likely BRCA candidate. axillary_lymphadenopathy on US (one 1.4 cm node — needs core biopsy / FNA). Pre-staging mammographic_abnormality reviewed; bilateral mammo with 3D tomosynthesis + MRI breast both done. No contralateral lesions, no multifocal disease. BMI 28, no DM, no cardiac comorbidity. eGFR 92 (preserved). Needs staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis + germline panel + Oncotype-DX (luminal-A vs luminal-B distinction matters for chemo-omission decision) + sentinel-LN biopsy planning. Receptor confirmation on surgical specimen (definitive ER/PR/HER2) before final Tx. Workup at the post-histology / pre-staging stage.

history.family_history_breast_ovarian: mother breast Ca age 51, maternal aunt ovarian Ca age 47

label: Diagnostic · Multi-system suspicion · Renal mass + atypical melanocytic + endometrial thickening
label_en: Diagnostic · Multi-system suspicion · Renal mass + atypical melanocytic + endometrial thickening

json._comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 56F postmenopausal with concurrent multi-system findings: 4.0 cm right renal mass (incidental on CT) + atypical pigmented dorsal-foot lesion (1.1 cm, ABCDE+) + endometrial thickening 12 mm on TVUS (clinically asymptomatic). Multi-modality concurrent suspicion across GU+skin+gyn. Engine should match WORKUP-SOLID-GENITOURINARY-SKIN-GYN (combined workup) and emit Diagnostic Brief — coordinated biopsy planning + germline panel (HRR + Lynch + BAP1 + others for multi-cancer susceptibility) + tumor-board consensus on biopsy sequencing.

suspicion.presentation: 56F postmenopausal × 4 yrs presents with multi-system concerning findings discovered during workup of unrelated symptoms: (1) Workup of microscopic hematuria revealed 4.0 cm contrast-enhancing exophytic kidney_mass right upper pole on CT (RENAL nephrometry 8); (2) Routine dermatology screening identified atypical pigmented growth on dorsal foot 1.1 cm with ABCDE positive (asymmetry + irregular border + variegated color + diameter + recent enlargement) — melanoma_suspect; (3) TVUS during postmenopausal cervical screening showed endometrial_thickening 12 mm without clinical symptoms (no PMB) — endometrial workup also indicated. PCP appropriately concerned for synchronous multi-primary cancers vs metastatic disease vs hereditary cancer syndrome. No family history of specific cancers but limited family knowledge. Workup needs coordinated biopsy planning across 3 sites + germline panel for multi-cancer susceptibility (BAP1, BRCA1/2, HRR, Lynch).
